sourceShortSummaries: The party confirms Eidrikas is truly himself, learns what the stolen stone did to the chasm mechanism, and commits to descending after the orcs.
The party edges the floating stone into the upper chamber, tests the blue column and shifting gravity, and finds a way down to the next level.
In the next chamber down, the party wakes a broken custodian automaton and learns only that repairs cannot resume until the threat below is resolved.
The party abandons the lightning-filled route, struggles back out, repositions the floating stone to the lowest level, and peers into the ruined bottom chamber.
Dropping through the hatch, the party opens the battle with Hypnotic Pattern, locks down most of the lesser orcs, and begins cutting through the ones still active.
The fight turns messy once the lightning orc rejoins the battle, fear effects start spreading, and the god caller becomes the center of the melee.
After enduring the god caller's lightning and terror effects, the party kills the last lightning orc, survives its explosion, and finally brings the god caller down.
The in-world aftermath is pushed to next session, and the transcript shifts entirely into scheduling and campaign planning.
sourceLongSummaries: From the floating rock beside the broken runic platform, the party studies Eidrikas, the dead from the earlier battles, and the strange cylindrical structure hanging below. Ekko confirms his mentor is genuine, Eidrikas explains that the orcs stole a black runic stone from the mechanism, warns them about the large Thark-marked leader among the survivors, and sends them down to recover the piece before the failing structure overwhelms him.

After positioning their floating rock so they can retreat if needed, the party surveys the top open chamber of the cylindrical structure. They discover the blue column rising through the room, realize the gravity shifts around it, recover the hanging rope, and work out how to descend through the cracked floors into the chamber below without getting smashed like the dead orc nearby.

The next level reveals crystal structures, floor hatches, and a damaged stone-and-metal automaton still trying to perform its duties. Finnan questions it in Giant, confirms that the orcs who came through were unauthorized, and keeps pressing for help, but the broken custodian can only repeat that repair functions are suspended until the active threat condition is removed.

Unable or unwilling to tackle the lightning-filled conduit room directly, the party fights its way back up through the heavy gravity, regroups in the upper chamber, and drags the floating stone around to the larger bottom section of the structure. Opening the final hatch reveals a wrecked chamber below, a cluster of arguing orcs, and a separate lightning-shrouded orc off to one side, setting up the ambush.

The ambush starts with Finnan's Hypnotic Pattern catching several of the orcs beneath the hatch while the party focuses the few enemies still moving. As the fight takes shape, the group identifies a Thark-marked god caller among the orcs, tries to avoid waking the entranced enemies, and begins thinning the field before the separate lightning-shrouded orc finally rushes in and one of the sleepers is shaken awake.

The command spell buys only a moment before the lightning-wreathed orc crackles, flees, and then loops back into the fight, while surviving lesser orcs continue to press the party at close range. The god caller's battle cries and curses start frightening Ekko and Eolo, the group scrambles to finish off the remaining support enemies, and by the end of the beat the battle has narrowed toward the god caller as the real problem.

The final phase opens with the god caller blasting the field with lightning, renewing his terror curse, and refusing to slow down even as most of his allies fall. Justas destroys the last nearby lightning orc and is caught in its violent death burst, but the party keeps pouring damage into the god caller until Eolo's arrows finally drop him for good, leaving only cleanup and the promised search for the stolen stone.

Once the DM calls the combat aftermath for next time, the session leaves the fiction behind and turns into scheduling, pacing, and discussion of how many Chasm sessions remain before the next campaign transition. No further in-world actions are played out, though the party is left poised to search the chamber and recover the stolen stone when play resumes.

locations.name: Great Chasm
locations.contextHint: floating rock beside Eidrikas's tilted runic platform above the suspended cylindrical structure

locations.name: Great Chasm suspended structure
locations.contextHint: climbing out, repositioning the floating stone, and opening the last hatch into the larger lower section
